 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.2.4","title":"Reduce heredoc trigger false positives","description":"Refine heredoc/inline trigger detection to avoid unnecessary Tier 2/3 scans.","notes":"Scope:
- Analyze current RegexSet triggers for overly broad matches (e.g., false triggers inside commit messages).
- Add safe-string gating to triggers where possible without introducing false negatives.
- Add targeted tests for known false-positive cases.
- Logging: include trigger id and suppression reason in verbose/e2e logs.

Acceptance criteria:
- Trigger recall remains 100% for true heredoc/inline code.
- Measurable reduction in accidental trigger rate in regression corpus.
- E2E regression harness confirms no unsafe allows.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:34:15.685521808-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T16:49:03.502679122-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.2.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:34:15.687101273-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.2.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:41:45.71744283-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-p9e","title":"Implement Terraform extractor (provisioners)","description":"## Objective

Implement an extractor for Terraform files to detect executable provisioner contexts.

## File Pattern

- `*.tf`

## Extraction Targets

### Provisioner Blocks (executed)
- `local-exec` command
- `remote-exec` inline commands
- `null_resource` provisioners

### NOT Extracted (data only)
- Variable definitions
- Output values
- Resource attributes (non-provisioner)
- Comments

## Example

```hcl
resource "null_resource" "cleanup" {
  provisioner "local-exec" {
    command = "rm -rf /tmp/*"  # EXTRACT THIS
  }
}

variable "dangerous" {
  default = "rm -rf /"  # DO NOT extract
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Correctly extracts provisioner commands with line numbers
- Handles HCL2 syntax (Terraform 0.12+)
- Ignores non-executable blocks
- Unit tests with representative fixtures","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Extract only from truly executed shell contexts (e.g., `local-exec`, `remote-exec`), not from:
  - documentation strings
  - variable defaults
- Negative fixtures must include data-only mentions of destructive commands.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:57.275380054-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T13:11:26.177891816-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T13:11:26.177891816-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Terraform provisioner extractor with local-exec/remote-exec support, HCL2 parsing, heredoc handling. Added to scan flow. 6 tests pass. Committed in a6b640d.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-p9e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:20.549018763-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-p9e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:46:12.516806682-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-perf","title":"Implement performance benchmarks for heredoc detection","description":"# Performance Benchmark Suite

## Objective

Create benchmarks to measure and enforce performance budgets for heredoc detection.

## Performance Budgets

| Operation | Budget | Panic Threshold |
|-----------|--------|----------------|
| Tier 1 regex check | < 10μs | > 100μs |
| Heredoc extraction | < 500μs | > 2ms |
| Language detection | < 50μs | > 200μs |
| AST parsing (small) | < 2ms | > 10ms |
| AST parsing (large) | < 10ms | > 50ms |
| Full pipeline | < 15ms | > 50ms |

## Benchmark Implementation

Using criterion for statistical benchmarks:

```rust
// benches/heredoc_perf.rs
use criterion::{criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion, BenchmarkId};

fn bench_tier1_regex(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let cases = [
        ("simple", "git status"),
        ("heredoc_marker", "python3 << 'EOF'\nprint('hi')\nEOF"),
        ("inline_script", "bash -c 'echo hello'"),
        ("long_command", &"x".repeat(10000)),
    ];

    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("tier1_regex");
    for (name, cmd) in cases {
        group.bench_with_input(
            BenchmarkId::new("might_contain_heredoc", name),
            cmd,
            |b, cmd| b.iter(|| might_contain_heredoc(cmd)),
        );
    }
    group.finish();
}

fn bench_full_pipeline(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark complete heredoc check flow
}

criterion_group!(benches, bench_tier1_regex, bench_full_pipeline);
criterion_main!(benches);
```

## Cargo.toml Addition

```toml
[[bench]]
name = "heredoc_perf"
harness = false

[dev-dependencies]
criterion = "0.5"
```

## CI Integration

Add to CI pipeline:

```yaml
- name: Run benchmarks
  run: cargo bench --bench heredoc_perf -- --noplot
```

## Regression Detection

Store baseline and compare:

```bash
# Save baseline
cargo bench --bench heredoc_perf -- --save-baseline main

# Compare against baseline
cargo bench --bench heredoc_perf -- --baseline main
```

## Test Data

Create fixtures:

- `benches/fixtures/small_heredoc.txt` (10 lines)
- `benches/fixtures/medium_heredoc.txt` (100 lines)
- `benches/fixtures/large_heredoc.txt` (1000 lines)
- `benches/fixtures/real_world_samples/` (actual heredocs from the wild)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Benchmarks exist for Tier 1 trigger, extraction, and at least one AST match path.
- A baseline is established and CI can detect meaningful regressions (thresholds documented).
- Benchmarks are deterministic enough to be useful (noise handled via criterion baselines).
- Fixture sizes cover small/medium/large and include at least one “worst-case” style input.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-phpp","title":"Define PHP destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"# PHP Destructive Patterns for Heredoc Scanning

## Objective

Define PHP heredoc matchers focused on **irreversible operations** (filesystem deletion and literal destructive shell payloads), while minimizing false positives.

## Scope boundary

- Focus on destructive outcomes.
- Do not include broad “dynamic code execution” patterns (`eval`, dynamic include) as default denies; too common and not necessarily destructive.

## Pattern families (v1)

### 1) Shell execution with destructive payload (high signal)

- `system("rm -rf ...")` / `` `rm -rf ...` `` (literal)
- `exec("git reset --hard ...")` (literal)

### 2) Recursive / high-risk filesystem operations

- custom recursive delete helpers are hard to statically detect; start with high-signal built-ins:
  - `unlink($path)` / `rmdir($path)`

Severity guidance:

- `Error` only when `$path` is a clearly catastrophic literal target.
- Otherwise `Warning`.

### 3) Obvious destructive SQL literals (optional)

If we can match literal `DROP` / `TRUNCATE` strings passed to `query/exec`, tag as `Warning` by default.

## False positive mitigation

- Do not flag `exec($VAR)` as deny.
- Require negative fixtures for:
  - harmless use of `exec` (e.g., `exec("php -v")`)
  - code printing dangerous strings

## Acceptance Criteria

- Defines a PHP pattern set aligned with severity taxonomy and focused on destructive outcomes.
- Includes at least 5 positive and 5 negative fixtures.
- No default hard denies unless the matched operation is clearly catastrophic.
","notes":"PHP patterns implemented in ast_matcher.rs. Language detection added to heredoc.rs, cli.rs, evaluator.rs. 13 test fixtures added (8 positive, 5 negative). Tests pass locally but build conflicts with concurrent WIP from other agents on trace.rs (skipped_due_to_budget field).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:00:00-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T01:21:32.812912548-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T01:21:32.812912548-05:00","close_reason":"PHP patterns implemented: 9 patterns in ast_matcher.rs, language detection in heredoc.rs, labels in cli.rs/evaluator.rs, 13 test fixtures. Build blocked by concurrent WIP in trace.rs/suggestions.rs.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-phpp","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:46:46.424057305-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-phpp","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:52:01.795976467-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-po7","title":"[PACK] cicd.jenkins - Jenkins Pack","description":"# Jenkins Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Jenkins CLI and API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `jenkins-cli delete-job` - Deletes jobs
- `jenkins-cli delete-node` - Deletes build nodes
- `jenkins-cli delete-credentials` - Deletes credentials
- `jenkins-cli delete-builds` - Deletes build history
- `curl -X POST .../doDelete` - REST API deletion

## Safe Patterns
- `jenkins-cli list-jobs`
- `jenkins-cli get-job`
- `jenkins-cli build` (trigger)
- `jenkins-cli who-am-i`
- `curl` GET requests to Jenkins

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: cicd.* (git_safety_guard-0v8)
- Keywords: jenkins-cli
- Note: Many orgs use custom Jenkins CLI wrappers

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Job deletion blocked
- [ ] Credential deletion blocked
- [ ] Node operations handled
- [ ] REST API patterns detected","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- Add unit tests for each destructive pattern plus safe/benign commands (false-positive cases).
- Add e2e corpus entries in the shared harness with verbose logging; ensure pack_id/pattern_name appear in logs.
- Use the pack unit test template (git_safety_guard-i79) and logging infra (git_safety_guard-jdb).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:31:28.246062425-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:47:06.591230026-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:47:06.591230026-05:00","close_reason":"Added Jenkins CLI/API pack with delete patterns, safe patterns, tests, registry updates","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-po7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.817663429-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-po7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.858089301-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-po7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.898414153-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-po7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.937991368-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-po7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.97802926-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-po7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.016856364-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-po7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.674097567-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-po7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.7204562-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
